import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let trimmed = string.trim(s)
  let n = string.len(trimmed)
  mut i = 0
  mut current = ""
  mut has = false
  while i < n
    let bopt = string.byte_at(trimmed, i)
    match bopt
    when Some(c)
      if c == 32 or c == 9
        if has
          match string.parse_int(current)
          when Some(v)
            result = list.push(result, v)
          when None
            ()
          end
          current = ""
          has = false
        end
      else
        current = "#{current}#{string.from_byte(c)}"
        has = true
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    match string.parse_int(current)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

let line1 = io.read_line()
let line2 = io.read_line()

match line1
when Some(l1)
  match line2
  when Some(l2)
    let nums = parse_ints(l1)
    match string.parse_int(string.trim(l2))
    when Some(t)
      let n = list.len(nums)
      mut found = false
      mut fi = 0
      mut fj = 0
      mut i = 0
      while i < n and not found
        mut j = i + 1
        while j < n and not found
          match list.get(nums, i)
          when Some(a)
            match list.get(nums, j)
            when Some(b)
              if a + b == t
                fi = i
                fj = j
                found = true
              end
            when None
              ()
            end
          when None
            ()
          end
          j = j + 1
        end
        i = i + 1
      end
      if found
        print("found=#{fi},#{fj}")
      else
        print("none")
      end
    when None
      print("none")
    end
  when None
    print("none")
  end
when None
  print("none")
end
